title: Senior AI Engineer II
company_focus:
Vertical software and insurance platform for homebuyers and
homeowners
description:
Porch Group is a leading vertical software and insurance
platform and is positioned to be the best partner to help
homebuyers move, maintain, and fully protect their homes. We
offer differentiated products and services, with homeowners
insurance at the center of this relationship. We
differentiate and look to win in the massive and growing
homeowners insurance opportunity by

1) providing the best services for homebuyers

2) led by advantaged underwriting in insurance

3) to protect the whole home

As a leader in the home services software-as-a-service
(“SaaS”) space, we’ve built deep relationships with
approximately 30 thousand companies that are key to the
home-buying transaction, such as home inspectors, mortgage
companies, and title companies.

In 2020, Porch Group rang the Nasdaq bell and began trading
under the ticker symbol PRCH. We are looking to build a
truly great company and are JUST GETTING STARTED.

Job Title: Senior AI Engineer II Location: India Workplace
Type: Remote

Job Summary

The future is bright for the Porch Group, and we’d love for
you to be a part of it as our Senior AI Engineer II

As a Senior AI Engineer II, you will be partnering with
product managers, software engineers, and data labelers to
derive value from our ever increasing data set of home and
consumer data sources, particularly by extracting property
attributes from text and image data. Your team has autonomy
to evolve the projects and platform with modern ideas and
practices and will continue to streamline model building and
deployment allowing rapid turnaround from concept to
deployment. The current Machine Learning stack is based on
Python, using Feast, Flyte, and BentoML among other tools to
allow the team to focus more energy on solving business
problems.

What You’ll Do As A Senior AI Engineer II

- Own the full machine learning lifecycle from research to
  production, leveraging Computer Vision and NLP techniques
  (e.g. BERT, ViT) to extract insights from unstructured
  data
- Evaluate and integrate modern AI and foundation model
  capabilities (e.g., vision-language models, embeddings, or
  retrieval-augmented approaches) into production systems
  where appropriate.
- Partner with product, operations, and engineering to
  translate business problems into high-impact machine
  learning or AI opportunities
- Advocate for sustainable tradeoffs, architecting solutions
  that balance product needs and long term goals
- Design experiments to quantify the business impact of
  machine learning models
- Collaborate with other MLEs on tooling improvements for
  model training and deployment
- Utilize our MLOps stack to drive feature engineering, model
  training, and data analysis
- Write robust, production-grade Python code to
  productionalize feature engineering, model training, and
  deployment
- Participate in the on-call rotation to assist engineers in
  troubleshooting

What You Will Bring As A Senior AI Engineer II

- Bachelor's or master's degree in computer science,
  engineering, or a related field (or four years of
  equivalent work experience)
- Experience: 7+ years of experience in machine learning and
  applied statistics, with a strong command of fundamental
  concepts
- A curious and driven individual who likes to work in a
  trustful, respectful, and collaborative environment
- Proven track record of leading high-impact ML initiatives
  from concept to production, demonstrating the
  architectural foresight to deliver immediate business
  value while championing the technical sustainability of
  the end-to-end ML lifecycle
- Experience deploying, monitoring, and A/B testing models in
  production
- Ability to communicate effectively in both business and
  highly technical contexts
- Demonstrated ability to write clean, modular, and testable
  code, applying software engineering best practices
- Experience working with relational databases and cloud
  solutions (e.g. Postgres, Google BigQuery, Google
  Datastore, and Google Cloud Storage)
- Experience in python data stacks, e.g. Numpy, Scipy, Pandas,
  Scikit-Learn and Matplotlib
- Proven experience working with US based business teams.
- Excellent written and communication skills in English
  language.
- Must currently reside in or are located within 150 km of
  Greater Delhi NCR, Greater Bangalore, or Greater Pune
- Working Hours: 8 Hours (Excluding breaks)  4 Core US Hours
  Overlap (7:30pm- 11:30pm IST)  4 flexible hours (IST)
- Workspace: A quiet space to work, an internet connection of
  at least 30 Mbps download | 10 Mbps upload

The application window for this position is anticipated to
close in 2 weeks (10 business days) from December 30, 2025.
Please know this may change based on business and
interviewing needs.

What You Will Get As A Porch Group Team Member

Pay Range*: INR 4,050,000 - 5,670,000 INR annually

*Please know your actual pay at Porch will reflect a number
of factors among which are your work experience and
skillsets, job-related knowledge, alignment with market and
our Porch employees, as well as your geographic location.

Our benefits package will provide you with comprehensive
coverage for your health, life, and financial well-being.

- Our benefits include medical insurance, accident insurance
  and retiral benefits.
- Our wellness programs include 12 company-paid holidays, 2
  flexible holidays, privilege/earned leave, casual/sick
  leave, paid maternity and paternity Leaves, and weekly
  wellness events.

#LI-DG1 #LI-REMOTE

What’s next?

Submit your application below and our Talent Acquisition
team will be reviewing your application shortly! If your
resume gets us intrigued, we will look to connect with you
for a chat to learn more about your background, and then
possibly invite you to have virtual interviews. What's
important to call out is that we want to make sure not only
that you're the right person for us, but also that we're the
right next step for you, so come prepared with all the
questions you have!

Porch is committed to building an inclusive culture of
belonging that not only embraces the diversity of our people
but also reflects the diversity of the communities in which
we work and the customers we serve. We know that the
happiest and highest performing teams include people with
diverse perspectives that encourage new ways of solving
problems, so we strive to attract and develop talent from
all backgrounds and create workplaces where everyone feels
seen, heard and empowered to bring their full, authentic
selves to work.

Porch is an Equal Opportunity employer. All qualified
applicants will receive consideration for employment without
regard to race, color, religion, sex including sexual
orientation and gender identity, national origin,
disability, protected veteran status, or any other
characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations,
and ordinances.

Computer vision for property image analysis and feature extraction
NLP-based extraction of property information from unstructured text documents
Vision-language model applications for home insurance underwriting
Deriving value from home and consumer data sources for insurance and home services
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves owning the full ML lifecycle from
research to production, directly building and deploying
models using Computer Vision, NLP (BERT, ViT), and
foundation models. The engineer will evaluate and integrate
vision-language models, embeddings, and RAG approaches into
production systems - all hands-on AI/ML model work.
